title: sinomalus halliana
side_effects: Sinomalus halliana can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to skin rashes, itching, and redness, while inhaling its particles may trigger respiratory issues like coughing and wheezing. Improper handling of the Sinomalus halliana plant can also result in eye irritation and potentially permanent damage to the eyesight.
